protocol.name: ext_input_trigger_registration_v1
protocol.copyright:

description.summary: Registration of global input triggers and associated actions

protocol.description:
This protocol lets a privileged client define global input actions and bind them to input triggers, such as key combinations. An action is an activity (for example "play/pause" or "toggle-launcher"); a trigger is an input, such as "Meta+p", that fires it. When a trigger fires, the client subscribed to the action is notified through the `ext_input_trigger_action_v1` protocol.
The registering client reserves a trigger (obtaining an `ext_input_trigger_v1`), creates an action (obtaining an `ext_input_trigger_action_control_v1` and, with it, a token), and associates triggers with the action. It then delivers the token to interested clients out of band (for example through a desktop settings service), which subscribe using `ext_input_trigger_action_manager_v1.get_input_trigger_action`.
For example, binding "Meta+p" to a "play/pause" action:
# bind the manager global and reserve a key combination manager = registry.bind(ext_input_trigger_registration_manager_v1) trigger = manager.register_keyboard_sym_trigger(Meta, XKB_KEY_p)
# create the action and collect its token control = manager.get_action_control("play/pause") on control.done(token): control.add_input_trigger_event(trigger) # hand the token to interested clients out of band settings_service.publish("play/pause", token)
# when the user later removes the binding, purge the action on user_removes_binding("play/pause"): control.cancel_and_destroy()

interface.name: ext_input_trigger_registration_manager_v1

description.summary: used to register input triggers and actions

interface.description: This global interface allows a Wayland client to register ownership of input trigger events and associate them with actions. It is advertised by the registry and bound by the client.

enum.name: capability

description.summary: the types of inputs that support triggers

enum.description: This is a bitmask of capabilities this system has; if a member is set, then triggers can be set.

entry.name: keyboard
entry.summary: key combinations can be used as triggers

event.name: capabilities
event.description: This is emitted when `ext_input_trigger_registration_manager_v1` is bound and whenever a system gains or loses the capabilities. The argument is a capability enum containing the complete set of capabilities.

arg.name: capabilities
arg.summary: types of inputs that support triggers

request.name: destroy

description.summary: destroy input trigger registration manager object

request.description: This informs the compositor that the input trigger registration manager object will no longer be used. Existing objects created through this interface remain valid.

enum.name: modifiers
enum.description: The various modifiers on the keyboard.

request.name: register_keyboard_sym_trigger
request.description:
This request creates an input trigger and asks the compositor to reserve it to the manager. This allows triggering on a character key combination such as "Meta+a".
As the layout changes, key locations might change. For example, when changing between a QWERTY and AZERTY layout, the location of the "Q" key will change, and the user will have to press a different physical key combination. With non-latin layouts, some key combinations might not be available.
The trigger begins when the combination of modifiers and keysym are pressed and ends when that changes. The keysym parameter is interpreted according to xkbcommon-keysyms.h.
The compositor will send either the `ext_input_trigger_v1.done` or `ext_input_trigger_v1.failed` event on the created object in response to this request.
Input actions triggered by this request will result in an `ext_input_trigger_action_v1.begin` event when the key combination is pressed and `ext_input_trigger_action_v1.end` when the key combination changes. Key repeats are ignored.

arg.name: modifiers
arg.summary: bitmask of modifier keys held for the trigger

arg.name: keysym
arg.summary: the key to trigger the action

request.name: register_keyboard_code_trigger
request.description:
This request creates an input trigger and asks the compositor to reserve it to the manager. This allows triggering on a specific key combination such as "Alt+the-key-above-tab".
The trigger begins when the combination of modifiers and keycode are pressed and ends when that changes.
The compositor will send either the `ext_input_trigger_v1.done` or `ext_input_trigger_v1.failed` event on the created object in response to this request.
Input actions triggered by this request will result in an `ext_input_trigger_action_v1.begin` event when the key combination is pressed and `ext_input_trigger_action_v1.end` when the key combination changes. Key repeats are ignored.

arg.name: modifiers
arg.summary: bitmask of modifier keys held for the trigger

arg.name: keycode
arg.summary: the key to trigger the action

request.name: get_action_control

description.summary: get input trigger action control object

request.description:
This request creates an action control object.
The name parameter describes the action (it has no semantic content and may be duplicated across actions).
The compositor will send the ext_input_trigger_action_control_v1.done event in response to this request.

interface.name: ext_input_trigger_v1
interface.description:
This object represents a client's request to reserve an input trigger. In response to its creation the compositor must send either the `done` or the `failed` event.
The done event indicates the trigger is now owned by the manager: the compositor will not allow this or any other manager to register the same trigger. The reservation lasts until the object is destroyed, which releases the trigger.
The failed event indicates the trigger could not be owned by the manager, for example because it is already reserved. The object is then of no further use and should be destroyed.

request.name: destroy
request.description: This informs the compositor that the input trigger will no longer be used.

event.name: done
event.description: This client is now responsible for handling this input trigger.

event.name: failed
event.description: The failed event indicates that the trigger is not "owned" by the manager. This means the object is of no further use and should be destroyed.

interface.name: ext_input_trigger_action_control_v1
interface.description:
This object represents an input trigger action owned by this client. The action associates one or more input triggers with a token that other clients can use, via the `ext_input_trigger_action_v1` protocol, to be notified when the action fires.
In response to the creation of this object the compositor must send the done event, which delivers the action's token. Triggers are associated with the action using `add_input_trigger_event` and `drop_input_trigger_event`.
Destroying the object with `cancel_and_destroy` purges the action, while destroy leaves it intact; see those requests for details.

request.name: add_input_trigger_event

description.summary: add an input trigger to the action

request.description: This request adds an input trigger to those that activate the action.

request.name: drop_input_trigger_event

description.summary: drop an input trigger from the action

request.description: This request drops an input trigger from those that activate the action.

event.name: done
event.description:
This event delivers the action's token. It is sent once, immediately after the object is created.
The action token is usable by the `ext_input_trigger_action_manager_v1` protocol to get handles to actions via `ext_input_trigger_action_manager_v1.get_input_trigger_action`. The token can be used by this or another client (or clients).
The token expires a short, implementation-defined time after this event is sent, whether or not it has been used; using it does not extend its lifetime. A client should therefore use the token, or hand it to another client, promptly. After it expires, passing it to `ext_input_trigger_action_manager_v1.get_input_trigger_action` raises an `invalid_token` protocol error.

request.name: cancel_and_destroy

description.summary: purge the input trigger action and destroy this object

request.description:
This purges the input trigger action and destroys this object. It would typically be used when the user deletes the input trigger the action represents.
The action itself ceases to exist: every `ext_input_trigger_action_v1` that was created from this action's token (by this or any other client) is notified with an `ext_input_trigger_action_v1.unavailable` event and will receive no further begin or end events.
To destroy this object while leaving the action (and any `ext_input_trigger_action_v1` objects derived from it) intact, use destroy instead.

request.name: destroy

description.summary: destroy this object but leave the action intact

request.description:
This destroys this object without purging the input trigger action. The action continues to exist: any `ext_input_trigger_action_v1` created from its token (by this or any other client) is unaffected and keeps receiving begin and end events.
To purge the action and make those objects unavailable, use `cancel_and_destroy` instead.
